rulings.Case Title: RENETTA J NAIL et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Berry Berry As Coordinating Defense Counsel, Or In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Prohibiting Berry Berry From Engaging In Conduct Outside The Limits Of General Order 41
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF RENETTA NAIL Motion To Disqualify Berry & Berry As Coordinating Defense Counsel, Or In The Alternative, Motion For An Order Prohibiting Berry & Berry From Engaging In Conduct Outside The Limits Of General Order 41 IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 301 (JUDGE WOOLARD RECUSES HERSELF). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ASPECT SOFTWARE INC VS. STATE OF BOARD OF EQUALIZATION,AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION WAS CONSIDERED. PLAINTIFF EXHAUSTED ITS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. THE CONTRACT WAS NOT A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT. EDS WAS NOT MAKING OR SELLING A PRODUCT RELYING ON PLAINTIFF'S PATENTS OR COPYRIGHTS. SEE PRESTON V. STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 197. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO A REFUND BECAUSE IT CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT ITS CONTRACT IS A TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER AGREEMENT AND FALLS UNDER THE R&T 6011(c)(10) EXCEPTION. PLAINTIFF'S COMMERCE CLAUSE ARGUMENT FAILS BECAUSE THE SHIPPING OF THE SOFTWARE TO PLEASANTON SHOWS A SUBSTANTIAL NEXUS WITH CALIFORNIA, THE TAX IS FAIRLY APPORTIONED BASED ON THE CONTRACT AMOUNT, THERE IS NO DISCRIMINATION AGAINST INTERSTATE COMMERCE, AND IT IS FAIRLY RELATED TO THE SERVICES PROVIDED BY THE STATE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WHITLOCK VS. ST.MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, NO TIMELY OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S NOTICE OF UNAVAILABILITY DOES NOT EXTEND THE TIME TO OPPOSE THE MOTION. SEE CARL V. SUPERIOR COURT (2007) 157 CAL.APP.4TH 73. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JYOTSNA SABRINA BIRES, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS A VS. MERVYN FRANCIS BURKE et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANTS LEE ASSEO AND THOMAS PIER'S Third Amended Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security. MOTION DENIED. rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA GAVELLO et al VS. DONALD BROWN,MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration Of Donald M Brown, M.D. [Ccp 1281.2, 1281.7, And 1295]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT DONALD BROWN, MD'S Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration Of Donald M Brown, M.D. [CCP 1281.2, 1281.7, And 1295]. PETITION GRANTED. PATIENT'S AGREEMENT TO BIND THESE PLAINTIFFS ENFORCEABLE. SEE MORMILE V. SINCLAIR (1994) 21 CAL.APP.4TH 1508, CCP SECTIONS 1281.2(c) AND 1295. THIS ACTION STAYED AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. DISCOVERY SHALL BE COORDINATED BETWEEN THE STATE COURT ACTION AND THE ARBITRATION TO AVOID DELAY AND DUPLICATION OF DISCOVERY. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KRISTINA GAVELLO et al VS. DONALD BROWN,MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration;Request For Judicial Notice,
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, June 17, 2009, line 7, DEFENDANT TINA ENGLE, R.N.'S Joinder In Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration. PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION GRANTED. PATIENT'S AGREEMENT TO BIND THESE PLAINTIFFS ENFORCEABLE FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN DEFENDANT BROWN'S PETITION. ARBITRATION AGREEMENT ENCOMPASSES DEFENDANT ENGLE AS A HEALTH CARE PROVIDER. THIS ACTION STAYED AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. =(302/CWW)
